analysis.reasoning: Core message: Wang Yi states China is not the USSR nor seeking to be a second US; regardless of development level, China will never seek hegemony or expansionism and will follow a path of peaceful development. Evaluation: CH1 (The West is immoral, hostile and decadent) – Not promoted; no attack on the West, only a contrast with the US without moral condemnation. CH2 (China is a benevolent power) – Promoted; emphasizes peaceful development and renouncing hegemony/expansionism, portraying China as cooperative and non-threatening internationally. CH3 (Epic history) – Not present; no historical adversity or CCP-led resilience referenced. CH4 (Appropriate political system and honorable values) – Partially overlaps, but this narrative focuses on domestic system/values; the tweet speaks to China’s international posture (peaceful development, no hegemony), which aligns more with CH2 than a defense of domestic governance. Hence, not selected. CH5 (CCP succeeds) – Not present; no claims about domestic achievements or leadership success. CH6 (Culture/nature/heritage) – Not relevant. Conclusion: The tweet promotes CH2.
